description:
You are an R&D Manager tracking internal confidential projects. Your inbox contains public technical mailing list discussions that must be organized into a nested folder structure. Create a parent folder named 'Projects' containing three sub-folders: 'Project-Shield', 'Project-Mind', and 'Project-Grid'. Sort emails from the Inbox into these folders based on the following keyword mappings:

- Project-Shield (Access Control): 'whitelist', 'blacklist', 'allow', 'deny'
- Project-Mind (Machine Learning): 'bayes', 'bayesian', 'learn', 'train', 'probability'
- Project-Grid (Distributed Net): 'razor', 'pyzor', 'dcc', 'network', 'timeout'

If an email contains keywords for multiple projects, prioritize them in the order of Shield > Mind > Grid. Once the emails are sorted, draft an email to 'cto@company.com' with the subject 'Weekly Project Intelligence Update' and a body listing the three projects along with the count of emails filed into each.
